summary: Implementation plan for AI client init rules and web interaction improvements across four scopes: (1) three-tier activation model for rule loading (REC-1), (2) tree-sitter AST-based forensic scanning upgrade (REC-2, gated by WASM evaluation), (3) CLI fab approve command compensating for Dashboard zero-write positioning, and (4) Dashboard four-module architecture with Module A first batch (coverage heatmap + hit reason visualization).
approach: Bottom-up: shared schema changes first (TASK-001), parallel evaluation of WASM feasibility (TASK-002) and CLI command (TASK-004), then build AST implementation on confirmed WASM foundation (TASK-003), and finally Dashboard UI layer consuming the enriched RulesPayload (TASK-005). TASK-001 and TASK-004 are fully parallel. TASK-002 must complete before TASK-003. TASK-005 depends on TASK-001 for RulesPayload.description_stubs.
estimated_time: 4-6 hours total (TASK-001: 45min, TASK-002: 30min, TASK-003: 90min, TASK-004: 45min, TASK-005: 75min)

design_decisions.decision: activation.tier defaults to undefined (not 'path') in schema
design_decisions.rationale: Optional field with undefined means no schema migration needed for existing nodes; runtime treats undefined same as 'path'
design_decisions.tradeoff: Runtime must handle undefined tier gracefully; no guarantee existing nodes declare tier explicitly

design_decisions.decision: RulesPayload.description_stubs is optional array field (not required)
design_decisions.rationale: Clients that haven't updated to handle stubs won't break — undefined-safe access pattern
design_decisions.tradeoff: Dashboard must defensively handle missing field; adds conditional rendering complexity

design_decisions.rationale: 3.5MB WASM is non-trivial; confirming integration feasibility before writing 80-120 LOC prevents wasted implementation work
design_decisions.tradeoff: Adds one sequential step; acceptable given risk of WASM incompatibility

design_decisions.decision: CLI approve reuses server-side approveHumanLock() directly (not via HTTP)
design_decisions.rationale: human-lint.ts already establishes CLI→server direct import pattern; no network dependency for local dev
design_decisions.tradeoff: CLI package must import from server package; dependency edge must be verified

design_decisions.decision: Dashboard Module A implements S-complexity features only (coverage heatmap + hit reason)
design_decisions.rationale: Establishes four-module navigation skeleton without blocking on M/L features that depend on future tasks
design_decisions.tradeoff: B/C/D modules are stub placeholders only; full module value requires future tasks

data_flow.diagram: agents.meta.json → agentsMetaNodeSchema (activation.tier) → loadRulesForPath() → RulesPayload (with description_stubs) → Dashboard RuleTopology view → CoverageHeatmap + HitReasonPanel

transformations: minimatch glob filter for path tier

stages.output: RulesPayload { L0, L1, L2, human_locked_nearby, description_stubs? }

stages.input: RulesPayload via SSE + AgentsMeta via API

completeness.notes: All 4 scopes from task description covered: REC-1 (TASK-001), REC-2 (TASK-002+003), CLI approve (TASK-004), Dashboard Module A (TASK-005)

granularity.notes: Each task is 30-90 minutes scope; TASK-002 is XS but gates TASK-003 and must be separate

dependencies.notes: No circular deps; TASK-001→TASK-005, TASK-002→TASK-003; TASK-001 and TASK-004 are fully parallel

convergence_criteria.notes: All criteria are specific and testable; no vague language

constraint_compliance.notes: Zero write operations in Dashboard; backward compatibility in schema changes; follows citty pattern for CLI
